User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the AgriWise application, encompassing various roles such as Farmer, Expert, Government Authority, or Logistics Provider.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification and communication. (Note: Authentication handled by external system).

mobileNumber.description: The user's mobile phone number, used for identification and communication. (Note: Authentication handled by external system).

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

role.description: The primary role of the user within the application (e.g., Farmer, Expert, GovernmentAuthority, LogisticsProvider).

preferredLanguage.description: The user's preferred language for the application interface and voice assistant (e.g., en, hi, bho, bn, mr, kn, ta, te, ml, pa, ha).

registrationDate.description: The date and time when the user registered.

location.description: General geographical location of the user, such as a state or district, for contextual information.

Crop.title: Crop
Crop.description: Details about a specific agricultural crop.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Crop entity.

name.description: The common name of the crop (e.g., Wheat, Rice, Cotton).

description.description: A brief description or characteristics of the crop.

Pest.title: Pest
Pest.description: Details about a specific agricultural pest.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Pest entity.

name.description: The common name of the pest (e.g., Aphid, Whitefly, Stem Borer).

description.description: A detailed description of the pest, its life cycle, and impact.

symptomIds.description: References to Symptom entities associated with this pest. (Relationship: Symptom N:N Pest)

Symptom.title: Symptom
Symptom.description: Describes a general symptom that can affect crops, potentially indicating a pest or disease.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Symptom entity.

name.description: The common name or phrase describing the symptom (e.g., Leaf Yellowing, Stunted Growth, Holes in Leaves).

description.description: A detailed description of the symptom.

Remedy.title: Remedy
Remedy.description: Information about a specific remedy for crop issues, which can be chemical or traditional ('Desi Nuskha').

id.description: Unique identifier for the Remedy entity.

name.description: The name of the remedy (e.g., Neem Oil, Chlorpyrifos).

type.description: The type of remedy (e.g., Chemical, Traditional).

description.description: A brief description of what the remedy is.

ingredients.description: A list of ingredients for traditional remedies. (Not applicable for chemical remedies)

usageInstructions.description: Instructions on how to use the remedy.

caution.description: Important cautionary notes or safety measures for the remedy.

imageUrl.description: An optional URL to an image illustrating the remedy or its application.

DiagnosticRequest.title: DiagnosticRequest
DiagnosticRequest.description: A request made by a farmer for AI-driven crop or pest diagnosis.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DiagnosticRequest entity.

farmerId.description: Reference to the User (Farmer) who made the request. (Relationship: User 1:N DiagnosticRequest)

cropId.description: Reference to the Crop entity for which the diagnosis is requested. (Relationship: Crop 1:N DiagnosticRequest)

symptomIds.description: References to Symptom entities observed by the farmer. (Relationship: Symptom N:N DiagnosticRequest)

description.description: Additional descriptive text provided by the farmer about the issue.

imageUrls.description: URLs to images uploaded by the farmer for diagnostic analysis.

requestDate.description: The date and time when the diagnostic request was submitted.

DiagnosticSuggestion.title: DiagnosticSuggestion
DiagnosticSuggestion.description: An AI-generated suggestion for a diagnostic request, including proposed remedies and reasoning.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DiagnosticSuggestion entity.

diagnosticRequestId.description: Reference to the DiagnosticRequest this suggestion is for. (Relationship: DiagnosticRequest 1:N DiagnosticSuggestion)

suggestedRemedyIds.description: References to Remedy entities suggested by the AI. (Relationship: Remedy N:N DiagnosticSuggestion)

confidenceScore.description: A score indicating the AI's confidence in the accuracy of the suggestion (e.g., 0.0 to 1.0).

reasoning.description: The AI's explanation or reasoning behind the diagnostic suggestion.

suggestedDate.description: The date and time when the suggestion was generated.

isCertified.description: Indicates whether this diagnostic suggestion has been reviewed and certified by an agricultural expert.

certifiedByUserId.description: Reference to the User (Expert) who certified this suggestion, if 'isCertified' is true. (Relationship: User 1:N DiagnosticSuggestion)

certificationDate.description: The date and time when the suggestion was certified, if 'isCertified' is true.

MarketPriceRecord.title: MarketPriceRecord
MarketPriceRecord.description: A record of the market price for a specific crop at a particular location and time.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MarketPriceRecord entity.

cropId.description: Reference to the Crop entity for which the price is recorded. (Relationship: Crop 1:N MarketPriceRecord)

state.description: The state where the market price was recorded (e.g., Maharashtra, Uttar Pradesh).

district.description: The district where the market price was recorded.

marketName.description: The name of the specific market (e.g., APMC Pune) where the price was recorded.

pricePerUnit.description: The price of the crop per unit (e.g., 25.50).

unit.description: The unit of measure for the price (e.g., Rs/Kg, Rs/Quintal).

recordDate.description: The date when the market price was recorded.

MarketTrend.title: MarketTrend
MarketTrend.description: Analyzed trends for crop prices in a specific state.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MarketTrend entity.

cropId.description: Reference to the Crop entity for which the trend is analyzed. (Relationship: Crop 1:N MarketTrend)

state.description: The state for which the market trend is determined.

trendStatus.description: The status of the price trend (e.g., Rising, Stable, Falling).

averagePriceLast7Days.description: The average price of the crop over the last 7 days.

percentageChangeLast7Days.description: The percentage change in price over the last 7 days.

analysisDate.description: The date and time when the market trend was last analyzed.

PriceAlert.title: PriceAlert
PriceAlert.description: An alert generated for government authorities when a crop price deviates significantly from the average.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PriceAlert entity.

cropId.description: Reference to the Crop entity that triggered the alert. (Relationship: Crop 1:N PriceAlert)

state.description: The state in which the price alert was triggered.

alertThresholdPercentage.description: The percentage threshold (e.g., 20%) that triggered the alert.

currentPrice.description: The current price of the crop that caused the alert.

averagePrice.description: The calculated average price against which the current price was compared.

alertDate.description: The date and time when the alert was generated.

isResolved.description: Indicates whether the price alert has been resolved by authorities.

resolvedByUserId.description: Reference to the User (GovernmentAuthority) who resolved the alert, if 'isResolved' is true. (Relationship: User 1:N PriceAlert)

resolutionDate.description: The date and time when the alert was resolved, if 'isResolved' is true.

alertStatus.description: The current status of the alert (e.g., Triggered, Acknowledged, Resolved).

LogisticsProviderProfile.title: LogisticsProviderProfile
LogisticsProviderProfile.description: Specific profile information for a user who is a Logistics Provider.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LogisticsProviderProfile entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User entity for this logistics provider. (Relationship: User 1:1 LogisticsProviderProfile)

companyName.description: The name of the logistics company.

contactPerson.description: The name of the primary contact person at the company.

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number for the logistics provider.

email.description: The contact email address for the logistics provider.

address.description: The physical address of the logistics provider.

description.description: A description of the logistics provider's services, fleet, and service areas.

TransportVehicle.title: TransportVehicle
TransportVehicle.description: Details about a specific transport vehicle offered by a logistics provider.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TransportVehicle entity.

logisticsProviderId.description: Reference to the LogisticsProviderProfile offering this vehicle. (Relationship: LogisticsProviderProfile 1:N TransportVehicle)

vehicleType.description: The type of vehicle (e.g., Truck, Van, Mini-Truck).

loadCapacityKg.description: The maximum load capacity of the vehicle in kilograms.

pricePerKm.description: The price charged per kilometer for this vehicle.

minLoadKg.description: The minimum load capacity in kilograms for which the vehicle is economical (optional).

maxLoadKg.description: The maximum load capacity in kilograms, essentially same as loadCapacityKg but could be different for different pricing tiers (optional).

isActive.description: Indicates if the vehicle is currently active and available for bookings.

serviceAreas.description: A list of geographical areas (e.g., states or districts) where this vehicle operates.

SocialPost.title: SocialPost
SocialPost.description: A post made by a farmer in the Kisan Social Network feed, potentially including pest sightings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SocialPost entity.

authorId.description: Reference to the User (Farmer) who authored the post. (Relationship: User 1:N SocialPost)

content.description: The text content of the social post.

imageUrls.description: URLs to images included in the post.

postDate.description: The date and time when the post was published.

locationDescription.description: An optional descriptive location for the post (e.g., 'My farm in Punjab').

isPestSighting.description: Indicates if this post also serves as a pest sighting report.

pestId.description: Reference to the Pest entity, if 'isPestSighting' is true. (Relationship: Pest 1:N SocialPost)

cropId.description: Reference to the Crop entity affected, if 'isPestSighting' is true. (Relationship: Crop 1:N SocialPost)

sightingLatitude.description: The GPS latitude coordinate of the pest sighting, if 'isPestSighting' is true.

sightingLongitude.description: The GPS longitude coordinate of the pest sighting, if 'isPestSighting' is true.

SocialComment.title: SocialComment
SocialComment.description: A comment made on a social network post.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SocialComment entity.

postId.description: Reference to the SocialPost this comment belongs to. (Relationship: SocialPost 1:N SocialComment)

authorId.description: Reference to the User who authored the comment. (Relationship: User 1:N SocialComment)

content.description: The text content of the comment.

commentDate.description: The date and time when the comment was published.

NuskhaVote.title: NuskhaVote
NuskhaVote.description: A vote (upvote or downvote) cast by a user on a traditional remedy ('Desi Nuskha').

id.description: Unique identifier for the NuskhaVote entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User (Farmer) who cast the vote. (Relationship: User 1:N NuskhaVote)

remedyId.description: Reference to the traditional Remedy entity that was voted on. (Relationship: Remedy 1:N NuskhaVote)

voteType.description: The type of vote cast (e.g., Upvote, Downvote).

voteDate.description: The date and time when the vote was cast.

definition.description: Root collection for user profiles. The userId must match the authenticated user's ID (request.auth.uid). Contains the user's role for application logic, but security rules for roles are handled by separate marker collections (e.g., /roles_experts).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: A marker collection indicating that the user with 'userId' is an 'Expert'. Used for DBAC in security rules (existence check) to avoid 'get()' operations on the user profile.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the expert user.

definition.description: A marker collection indicating that the user with 'userId' is a 'Government Authority'. Used for DBAC in security rules (existence check).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the government authority user.

definition.description: A marker collection indicating that the user with 'userId' is a 'Logistics Provider'. Used for DBAC in security rules (existence check).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the logistics provider user.

definition.description: Master data collection for various agricultural crops. Publicly readable for all users.

params.name: cropId
params.description: The unique identifier of the crop.

definition.description: Master data collection for agricultural pests. Publicly readable for all users.

params.name: pestId
params.description: The unique identifier of the pest.

definition.description: Master data collection for crop symptoms. Publicly readable for all users.

params.name: symptomId
params.description: The unique identifier of the symptom.

definition.description: Master data collection for chemical and traditional ('Desi Nuskha') remedies. Publicly readable for all users.

params.name: remedyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the remedy.

definition.description: Private collection storing diagnostic requests made by a specific farmer. The 'farmerId' in the document's data matches the path parameter for ownership (request.auth.uid == farmerId).

params.name: farmerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the farmer making the diagnostic request.

params.name: diagnosticRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific diagnostic request.

definition.description: AI-generated suggestions for a specific diagnostic request. Includes denormalized 'farmerId' (the ID of the farmer who made the request) for authorization independence, allowing farmers to read their own suggestions and experts to certify them directly without 'get()' on the parent request.

params.name: farmerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the farmer associated with the parent diagnostic request.

params.name: diagnosticRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent diagnostic request.

params.name: suggestionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the diagnostic suggestion.

definition.description: Records of historical market prices for crops at various locations. Publicly readable for all users, enabling query access patterns.

params.name: recordId
params.description: The unique identifier of the market price record.

definition.description: Analyzed trends for crop prices. Publicly readable for all users, supporting general market intelligence queries.

params.name: trendId
params.description: The unique identifier of the market trend record.

definition.description: Alerts triggered for significant crop price deviations. Accessible only by Government Authorities (via '/roles_governmentAuthorities/{userId}' DBAC). Includes 'resolvedByUserId' and 'alertStatus' for direct status and ownership checks.

params.name: alertId
params.description: The unique identifier of the price alert.

definition.description: Profiles of logistics providers. Publicly readable for farmers to discover services. Includes 'userId' (the ID of the user who owns this profile) for authorization independence, ensuring only the owner can modify their profile.

params.name: logisticsProviderProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier for the logistics provider's profile.

definition.description: Details of transport vehicles offered by a specific logistics provider. Publicly readable. Includes denormalized 'logisticsProviderUserId' (the ID of the user owning the parent LogisticsProviderProfile) for authorization independence, allowing only the owner to manage their vehicles.

params.name: logisticsProviderProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent logistics provider's profile.

params.name: vehicleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the transport vehicle.

definition.description: Public social network posts by farmers. Publicly readable for community engagement. Includes 'authorId' for ownership, allowing authors to manage their own posts.

params.name: postId
params.description: The unique identifier of the social post.

definition.description: Comments on social network posts. Publicly readable. Includes 'postId' and 'authorId' (the commenter) for authorization, allowing commenters to manage their own comments.

params.name: postId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent social post.

params.name: commentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the comment.

definition.description: Records a user's vote on a traditional remedy ('Desi Nuskha'). Nested under the user to ensure uniqueness per user per remedy (remedyId serves as the document ID). The 'userId' in the path matches the owner.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user casting the vote.

params.name: remedyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the traditional remedy being voted on.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for AgriWise prioritizes Authorization Independence (CRITICAL), ensuring robust and debuggable security rules. This is achieved primarily through a strategic denormalization of authorization context and a clear structural segregation of data based on its security posture.

**Authorization Independence (Strategy A - Denormalization):**
1.  **Role-Based Access Control (DBAC):** Instead of relying on a `role` field within the main `/users/{userId}` document (which would require a `get()` call in security rules, violating authorization independence), dedicated top-level collections like `/roles_experts/{userId}`, `/roles_governmentAuthorities/{userId}`, and `/roles_logisticsProviders/{userId}` are introduced. The mere *existence* of a document at these paths signifies that `request.auth.uid` holds that specific role. This allows for simple `exists()` checks in security rules, eliminating cross-document `get()` dependencies for role verification.
2.  **Nested Data Ownership:** For subcollections where access depends on the parent document's owner, the owner's `userId` is explicitly denormalized into the subcollection's documents. For example:
    *   `DiagnosticSuggestion` documents (nested under `/users/{farmerId}/diagnosticRequests/{diagnosticRequestId}/suggestions`) will include a `farmerId` field. This allows rules to check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.farmerId` directly, without needing to `get()` the parent `DiagnosticRequest` document.
    *   `TransportVehicle` documents (nested under `/logisticsProviderProfiles/{logisticsProviderProfileId}/vehicles`) will include a `logisticsProviderUserId` field. This enables direct checks like `request.auth.uid == resource.data.logisticsProviderUserId`, bypassing the need to `get()` the parent `LogisticsProviderProfile`.
    *   `LogisticsProviderProfile` documents themselves will contain a `userId` field to link directly to the authenticated user for ownership checks.

This denormalization strategy ensures that security rules for a given document can be evaluated atomically, relying only on the data within that document (`resource.data`) and the authenticated user's ID (`request.auth.uid`), preventing complex and error-prone `get()` operations.

**Structural Segregation (Strategy B - Homogeneous Security Posture) & QAPs:**
All documents within a collection share the same security requirements, simplifying security rules and enabling efficient Query Access Patterns (QAPs):
*   **Public Reference Data:** Collections like `/crops`, `/pests`, `/symptoms`, `/remedies`, `/marketPriceRecords`, and `/marketTrends` are designed for public read access. Rules for these collections can simply allow `read: if true;`, facilitating secure `list` queries without complex filtering logic.
*   **User-Owned Private Data:** Data strictly owned and managed by individual users (e.g., `/users/{userId}/diagnosticRequests`, `/users/{userId}/nuskhaVotes`) is nested under the `/users/{userId}` path. This naturally enforces `request.auth.uid == userId` for all operations, making `list` queries for a user's own data inherently secure.
*   **Collaborative/Role-Restricted Data:**
    *   `/priceAlerts` are accessible only to `Government Authorities`, leveraging the `/roles_governmentAuthorities/{userId}` collection for DBAC. Listing all alerts is secure because all documents within this collection are intended for this role.
    *   `/socialPosts` and `/socialPosts/{postId}/comments` are publicly readable, enabling a dynamic community feed. Authors retain write/delete access to their own content through `resource.data.authorId` checks.
    *   `/logisticsProviderProfiles` and their nested `/vehicles` are publicly readable for discovery but restrict write access to the specific `Logistics Provider` who owns the profile, verified via the denormalized `userId`.

This design inherently supports QAPs because `list` operations will either retrieve publicly accessible data, data explicitly owned by the requesting user (enforced by the path), or data accessible to a specific role (enforced by the role collection existence check), without requiring the rules engine to act as a filter that might return partial results or incur high costs.
